class.name: FmodAudioStream
class.brief_description: A resource representing an audio stream for FMOD playback.
class.description: FmodAudioStream is a Resource that holds audio data for playback through the FMOD audio engine. It supports loading audio from files (including [code]res://[/code] paths) and provides configurable creation modes for streaming or sample-based playback. The stream can be played using [FmodAudioStreamPlayer] or used directly with FMOD's channel system. The underlying [FmodSound] object is created lazily when first requested via [method get_sound].

method.name: clear
method.description: Clears the audio data and releases the internal [FmodSound] resource. This frees up memory and resets the stream to an empty state. The stream will need to be reloaded before it can be played again.

method.name: get_length
method.description: Returns the length of the audio in seconds. Returns [code]0.0[/code] if the sound has not been created or if no data is loaded.

method.name: get_sound
method.description: Returns the [FmodSound] object for this stream, creating it if necessary (lazy creation). The sound is created using the current [member mode_flags] settings. If the mode flags have changed since the last call, the sound will be recreated with the new settings. Returns an empty reference if no audio data is loaded or if the FMOD system is not available.

method.name: invalidate_sound
method.description: Invalidates the cached [FmodSound] object while preserving the audio data. The next call to [method get_sound] will create a new sound with the current mode flags. This is useful when you need to change mode flags (e.g., switching between 2D and 3D) without reloading the audio data.

method.name: is_data_loaded
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if audio data has been loaded into this stream, [code]false[/code] otherwise.

method.name: load_from_file
method.description: Loads an audio stream from a file path. The path can be a Godot resource path (e.g., [code]"res://audio/music.mp3"[/code]) or an absolute file path. The [param flags] parameter specifies the creation mode flags (bitmask of [enum CreateMode]). Defaults to [constant MODE_STREAM] for streaming playback from disk. Returns an empty reference if the file cannot be opened or read.

method.name: preload
method.description: Creates the internal [FmodSound] immediately using the current [member mode_flags]. Use this before playback to move FMOD sound creation and stream prebuffering out of the first [method FmodAudioStreamPlayer.play] call. Returns [code]true[/code] if the sound is ready.

method.name: is_preloaded
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the internal [FmodSound] has already been created.

members.member: The raw audio data loaded from file. This is typically set by the audio importer or by [method load_from_file]. The data is stored in memory and used to create the FMOD sound object when [method get_sound] is called.

Creation mode flags that determine how the audio is loaded and played. This is a bitmask of [enum CreateMode] values. When these flags are changed, the internal [FmodSound] is released and will be recreated with the new flags on the next call to [method get_sound]. Default value is [constant MODE_STREAM] (1).

constants.constant: Default creation mode. No special flags are set.
Stream the audio from memory. The audio data is read progressively rather than being fully decoded at once. This reduces memory usage for large files but may have higher CPU overhead during playback.
Load the entire audio file as a sample into memory. This allows for faster playback and lower CPU usage, but consumes more memory. Best suited for short sound effects.
Enable normal loop playback. When the audio reaches the end, it will loop back to the beginning.
Enable bidirectional (ping-pong) loop playback. When the audio reaches the end, it will play backwards to the beginning, then forwards again, creating a seamless back-and-forth loop.

Force 2D mode, disabling all 3D processing for this sound. When set, the sound will play without any 3D spatial positioning, distance attenuation, or doppler effects. This is useful for UI sounds, background music, or when using custom 2D audio positioning.

Force 3D mode, enabling 3D spatial processing for this sound. When set, the sound will be processed with 3D positioning, distance attenuation, and other spatial effects. This is the default for 3D audio sources.
